            "text": "title: \"Digital Garden\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Digital Garden - A Wiki You Can Walk Through\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"A digital garden is a personal site where notes grow in public - linked, imperfect, alive. ideas.jorap.com is my version.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {

            "text": "tags: [\"Digital Garden\", \"PKM\", \"Publishing\", \"Wiki\", \"Second Brain\", \"TiddlyWiki\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "A blog is a timeline. A **digital garden** is a topology - ideas connected by meaning, not publish date. Some pages are seedlings. Some are mature. All can change without pretending the first version was final."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "That's the vibe I want for ideas.jorap.com alongside the polished posts on JoRap Notes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Publish before polish"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Garden pages can be short. **Perfection is optional; presence is not.** I'd rather have a rough note linked than a perfect note in Drafts forever."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Heavy linking, light taxonomy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Tags help entry points. Links help exploration. **Wander the graph** when you're thinking, not only when you're searching."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Changelog mindset"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Edit in place. Note what changed if it matters. Readers expect growth, not frozen articles."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "A digital garden is for thinkers who hate losing half-formed work. **Plant notes. Link them. Revisit.** The paths get interesting after a year, not a weekend setup sprint."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Advantages of Digital Gardens\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Why Digital Gardens Beat Perfect Blog Archives\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"A digital garden grows in public - messy, linked, alive. Here's why I prefer that over polishing every thought into a finished essay.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Digital Garden\", \"PKM\", \"Publishing\", \"Note Taking\", \"Second Brain\", \"Ideas\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"advantages-of-digital-gardens\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "Most blogs are graveyards of finished posts. A **digital garden** is closer to a yard you actually walk through - some beds are neat, some are overgrown, and paths connect ideas that wouldn't sit next to each other in a chronological feed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "I run JoRap Notes as a blog and ideas.jorap.com as a garden-ish wiki. Different jobs. The garden wins when I'm still figuring something out."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Work in progress is allowed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Blog posts feel like they need a conclusion. Garden notes can say \"I'm not sure yet\" and still be worth keeping. **That honesty saves me from fake certainty** and lets readers follow along while an idea develops."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Links beat categories"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Folders and tags help. **Bidirectional links help more** when you're exploring. You stumble from \"spaced repetition\" to \"slow productivity\" to \"why my Anki deck failed\" without planning a series."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Low friction publishing"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "A garden note can be three paragraphs and a link. No hero image crisis. No SEO title angst. Ship the thought, refine later. My barrier to \"put it somewhere\" drops when perfection isn't the gate."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Compounding over time"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Old garden notes resurface when you link new ones. A half-baked note from 2024 suddenly matters in 2026 because you connected it to something current. **Blogs archive by date. Gardens compound by connection.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "Digital gardens aren't for everyone. If you need every page polished, stay with traditional posts. If you think for a living and hate losing half-finished insights, **a garden is where ideas get to breathe before they're ready for the main stage.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 48
          },

        },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Atomic Design for Notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Atomic Design for Notes - Small Pieces, Big Reuse\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Brad Frost's atomic design is for UI components. I borrowed the idea for notes - one idea per file, combinable later. Here's how that changed my wiki.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Atomic Notes\", \"PKM\", \"Note Taking\", \"Zettelkasten\", \"Information Architecture\", \"Second Brain\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"atomic-design-for-notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "UI designers talk about atoms, molecules, organisms. My notes started as long documents - meeting dumps, mixed topics, useless six months later. **Atomic design for notes** means one clear unit per file, written so it can combine with others later."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Not smaller for the sake of smaller. Smaller so you can remix."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Atoms: one claim per note"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "An atomic note answers one question or states one idea. \"Why middle-click saves tab chaos.\" Not \"browser tips, meeting notes, and what I had for lunch.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "If I'm writing &#96;and&#96; too much, I'm probably merging atoms that should split."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "## Molecules: linked clusters"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "Two or three atomic notes plus a short connective note become a molecule - enough to draft a blog section or sermon point. **I don't write the essay first. I assemble it.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "My MOCs (maps of content) are often molecule-level index pages."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "## Organisms: publishable drafts"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "A blog post or client deliverable is the organism - built from atoms I already trust. The Hugo post you're reading might be ten tiddlers and a glue paragraph."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "Atomic design for notes isn't a branding exercise. It's **write once, reuse everywhere** without copy-pasting a messy Google Doc. Split the dump. Link the pieces. Ship when the organism is ready."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 46
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__atomic-design-for-notes.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3352915048599243
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Zettelkasten\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Zettelkasten - Linked Atomic Notes That Scale\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Zettelkasten is one idea per note, dense links, writing from the network. My digital version lives in TiddlyWiki and Markdown.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Zettelkasten\", \"PKM\", \"Atomic Notes\", \"Linking\", \"Luhmann\", \"Note Taking\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"zettelkasten\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "**Zettelkasten** (German \"slip-box\"): atomic notes, unique IDs optional, links between ideas, write by traversing connections. Not a product - **a method.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "I use zettelkasten principles without religious ID formats. Filename + links suffice."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Atomic notes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "One claim, standalone readable. **Split combined captures.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Link generously"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Each new note connects to at least one old. **Orphans get review or cut.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Write from the graph"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Assemble articles by following link chains. **Bottom-up composition.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Zettelkasten rewards writers who link while thinking. **Start with ten notes and ten links.** Scale when express proves the method."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__zettelkasten.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.32733333110809326
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# Content Management"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "Markdown under the language content dir, rendered via Hugo's content/layout pairing. Content lives at root: &#96;content/english/&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "## Architecture"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "Multilingual — each language sets &#96;contentDir&#96; in &#96;config/_default/languages.toml&#96; (English → &#96;content/english/&#96;). **List the dir before assuming the language** (e.g. &#96;content/english/blog/post.md&#96; → English, not &#96;content/blog/post.md&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "Common examples often found here include:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "- **Blog Posts**: &#96;blog/&#96; (posts + &#96;_index.md&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "- **Authors**: &#96;authors/&#96; (one file each + &#96;_index.md&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "- **Pages**: &#96;about/_index.md&#96;, &#96;contact/_index.md&#96;, &#96;pages/*.md&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "- **Reuseable Sections**: &#96;sections/*.md&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "## Frontmatter (common fields)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "Hugo reads what templates expect, so **mirror an existing file in the same section** rather than guessing."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- &#96;title&#96;: String. The main title of the post."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- &#96;date&#96;: ISO Date string (e.g., &#96;2022-04-04T05:00:00Z&#96;), use exact current time for &#96;date&#96; otherwise Hugo won't render it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "- &#96;description&#96;: String. Short summary used for lists and SEO."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- &#96;image&#96;: String. Path to the cover image (starts with &#96;/images/&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- &#96;draft&#96;: Boolean (&#96;true&#96;/&#96;false&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "## Section-specific common frontmatter:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "- **Blog post**: &#96;title&#96;, &#96;meta_title&#96;, &#96;description&#96;, &#96;date&#96;, &#96;image&#96;, &#96;categories[]&#96;, &#96;tags[]&#96;, &#96;author&#96;, &#96;draft&#96;. &#96;author&#96; must match an author &#96;title&#96; in &#96;authors/&#96;; &#96;categories&#96;/&#96;tags&#96; are taxonomies (links auto); &#96;draft: true&#96; hides in prod."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "- **Author**: &#96;title&#96;, &#96;email&#96;, &#96;image&#96;, &#96;description&#96;, &#96;social[]&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "- **any reuseable section** (&#96;sections/*.md&#96;): &#96;enable&#96;, &#96;title&#96;, &#96;image&#96;, &#96;description&#96;, &#96;button&#96; (&#96;enable&#96;/&#96;label&#96;/&#96;link&#96;), and &#96;build.render: \"never\"&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "## Naming, Images, i18n"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "- Kebab-case filenames → URL slug (&#96;my-post.md&#96; → &#96;/blog/my-post/&#96;). Section landing = &#96;_index.md&#96; (branch bundle); co-located resources = &#96;page/index.md&#96; (leaf bundle)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "- **Draft posts** (this site): prefix the filename with &#96;__&#96; (e.g. &#96;__my-post.md&#96;) and set &#96;draft: true&#96;. Add &#96;slug: \"my-post\"&#96; so preview URLs stay clean. To publish, rename without the prefix and set &#96;draft: false&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "- Images go under &#96;assets/images/&#96; and use image module &#96;{{ partial \"image\" (dict \"Src\" .image \"Alt\" \"...\" \"Loading\" \"eager\" \"Class\" \"...\" \"DisplayXL\" \"800x\") }}&#96; — don't hand-write &#96;\u003cimg\u003e&#96;."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 35
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.agents/skills/hugo-template-guidance/references/content-management.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.31936100125312805
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"How I Built JoRap Notes: My Journey from Expensive Hosting to Free (and Better!)\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"How I Built JoRap Notes: A Developer's Guide to Free Static Site Hosting\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"I lost my old PHP hosting (and a chunk of my work with it). A weekend of fumbling later, I landed on Hugo + GitHub + Cloudflare Pages - free, faster, and honestly better than what I was paying for.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "slug: \"how-i-built-jorap-notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "date: 2024-06-14T05:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "image: \"/images/joraps-world.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "categories: [\"Website\", \"Technology\", \"Tutorial\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "tags: [\"Website Building\", \"Free Hosting\", \"Hugo CMS\", \"GitHub\", \"Cloudflare Pages\", \"Static Sites\", \"JAMstack\", \"Self-Hosting\", \"PHP\", \"Migration\", \"Developer Life\", \"Hugo\", \"DevOps\", \"Site Migration\", \"Static Site Generator\", \"Web Hosting\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "\u003e **TL;DR**: I lost my old PHP hosting and a chunk of my work with it. After a weekend of fumbling around, I landed on Hugo + GitHub + Cloudflare Pages, and the result is genuinely better than what I was paying $12/month for. Here's exactly how I did it - including the parts I messed up."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "## The short backstory"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "For years I had a basic PHP hosting plan, about $12 a month, that I shared with a client. When that client moved on, suddenly I was paying the whole bill for what was effectively just my personal blog. It didn't make sense."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "I'd been hearing about static site generators and the whole \"JAMstack\" thing for a while but never had a reason to try them. Losing my hosting setup turned out to be exactly that reason. Sometimes you don't fix something until you have to."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## What I ended up using"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "Three pieces, all free, all working together:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "- **Hugo** generates the site."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "- **GitHub** stores the code."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "- **Cloudflare Pages** builds and serves it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "That's it. There's no database, no admin panel, no PHP, no cron jobs. When I want to publish, I write a Markdown file, push it to GitHub, and ninety seconds later it's live."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "### Hugo, in plain English"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Hugo takes a folder of Markdown files and a theme, and spits out a folder of plain HTML. That's the whole job. No database to back up, no server-side code to keep patched, nothing to break."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "Coming from years of WordPress, the speed difference was the first thing I noticed. Pages don't *load* - they just appear. There's nothing to wait for."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 36
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/how-i-built-jorap-notes.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.31552448868751526
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"The Garage Concept\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"The Garage Concept - Messy Workshop, Clean Showroom\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"The garage holds tools, scraps, experiments. The showroom is the blog. Don't confuse the two.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Garage Concept\", \"Digital Garden\", \"PKM\", \"Publishing\", \"Workflow\", \"Privacy\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"the-garage-concept\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "**The garage concept** for PKM: private wiki is the greasy garage - half projects, spare parts, notes named &#96;fix-this-weird-bug&#96;. **Public site is showroom** - finished, swept, labeled."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "ideas.jorap.com skews garage. JoRap Notes skews showroom. Both legitimate."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Permission to be messy privately"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Not every tiddler needs polish. **Garage notes speed thinking.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Clear promotion path"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Showroom pieces graduate from garage with edit pass. **One-way unless updating published truth.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Visitors see showroom"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Don't point clients at raw vault. **Obvious but violated often.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Garage concept reduces shame about messy notes. **Mess where it's useful; polish where it's public.** Two rooms, one house."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__the-garage-concept.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.31101369857788086
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Associative Linking\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Associative Linking - How Ideas Find Each Other\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Folders sort. Links think. Here's how I use associative linking so notes meet each other without me remembering every filename.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Linking\", \"PKM\", \"Zettelkasten\", \"Note Taking\", \"Second Brain\", \"Digital Garden\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"associative-linking\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "I used to file notes like laundry - whites here, colors there. Problem: **real ideas don't respect your drawer labels.** A thought about parenting shows up in a work note. A Hugo trick reminds you of a PKM principle."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Associative linking means you connect notes because they *relate*, not because they share a folder."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## One idea, many doors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "The same note can link from a project page, a MOC, and a random daily entry. **You don't have to pick one parent.** That's the win over strict hierarchies."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "In TiddlyWiki I use &#96;[[double bracket]]&#96; links. In Markdown repos, relative links or wiki-style aliases. The syntax matters less than the habit."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "## Link when it sparks, not when it's perfect"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "I link while writing - \"this reminds me of X\" - even if the target note is half empty. **Empty linked notes become prompts** for future writing. The graph fills in over months."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "## Backlinks are the reward"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "When you open a note and see five other pages pointing here, you understand why it mattered. Search finds needles. **Backlinks show you the haystack you built.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "Associative linking turns a folder of files into a thinking tool. Don't over-plan the graph. **Write the note, link the thought, move on.** The structure emerges when you revisit - not when you day-one architect it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 44
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__associative-linking.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.310125470161438
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "- **Performance optimization** - Making sites fast"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "- **SEO basics** - Getting found on Google"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "### Key concepts to understand:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "#### Static Site Generation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140,
            "text": "- **Traditional websites**: Server generates pages when users visit"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "- **Static websites**: All pages are pre-built as HTML files"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "- **Result**: Much faster loading, cheaper hosting, more secure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144,
            "text": "#### How Hugo Works"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "Your Markdown + Templates + Configuration → Hugo → Static HTML Website"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "#### Development vs Production"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "- **Development**: &#96;npm run dev&#96; - rebuilds on every change"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "- **Production**: &#96;npm run build&#96; - optimized for deployment"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "## 📁 Project Structure (What Files Do What)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "jorap.com/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "├── 📁 assets/           # Static assets (CSS, JS, images)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "│   ├── css/            # Custom stylesheets"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "│   ├── images/         # Your images (photos, logos, etc.)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "│   └── js/             # JavaScript files"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "├── 📁 config/          # Hugo configuration files"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "│   └── _default/       # Main configuration files"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "│       ├── hugo.toml       # Main configuration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "│       ├── params.toml     # Site parameters"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "│       ├── menus.en.toml   # Navigation menus"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "│       ├── languages.toml  # Language settings"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "│       └── module.toml     # Hugo modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "├── 📁 content/         # Markdown content files"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "│   └── english/        # English content"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "│       ├── _index.md       # Homepage content"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "│       ├── blog/           # Blog posts go here"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "│       ├── authors/        # Author profiles"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "│       ├── contact/        # Contact information"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "│       ├── pages/          # Static pages"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "│       └── sections/       # Reusable content sections"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "├── 📁 data/            # Data files (JSON/YAML)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179,
            "text": "│   ├── settings.json   # Site settings"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "│   ├── social.json     # Social media links"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "│   └── theme.json      # Colors and fonts"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "├── 📁 themes/          # Website design and layout"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "│   └── jorap/          # Custom theme"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 183
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 133
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3091188669204712
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Atomic Notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Atomic Notes - One Idea, One File\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Long notes age badly. I split ideas into atomic notes - single thoughts I can link, quote, and reuse without rereading a wall of text.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Atomic Notes\", \"Zettelkasten\", \"PKM\", \"Note Taking\", \"Second Brain\", \"Linking\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"atomic-notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "I found a 2022 note titled \"Productivity thoughts.\" Inside: a podcast quote, a grocery list, a Hugo bug, and something about prayer. **None of it was findable** because none of it was atomic."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "An atomic note holds one idea, named so future-you recognizes it. \"Middle-click opens tabs in background\" is atomic. \"Stuff from Tuesday\" is not."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## The split test"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Can you summarize this note in one sentence without using \"and\"? If not, split it. **Future-you is lazy.** One sentence at the top saves ten minutes of re-reading."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Write for reuse"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Atomic notes should make sense out of context - enough that you could paste one into a blog post or email. I add: source link, date, why it mattered to me. Three lines, not three pages."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Links do the heavy lifting"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Atoms connect into chains. You don't need one mega-document when **twelve small notes with backlinks** tell the same story with more flexibility."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Atomic notes feel fussy until you try to write from your archive. Then they're the difference between a library and a junk drawer. **One idea. One file. Link the rest.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__atomic-notes.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3058750629425049
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"From Note to Book\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"From Note to Book - Assembly Writing\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Books aren't written; they're compiled. My path from atomic notes to long-form is outline, link, expand.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Writing\", \"Books\", \"PKM\", \"Evergreen Notes\", \"Publishing\", \"Assembly\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"from-note-to-book\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "Nobody sits down and writes a book in one stream. They **assemble** - chapters from essays, essays from paragraphs, paragraphs from notes. My wiki is the bottom of that stack."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "If you're sitting on years of notes, you might be closer to a manuscript than you think."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## MOC as table of contents"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "A map of content for the topic becomes chapter list. **Each link is a section draft.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Expand atoms in passes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Pass one: glue paragraphs between notes. Pass two: voice and flow. Pass three: cut redundancy. **Don't rewrite from zero.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Hugo drafts as chapters"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "&#96;__book-chapter-03.md&#96; with &#96;draft: true&#96; until the arc works. Same pipeline as blog, longer horizon."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "From note to book is a logistics problem. **Organize the bricks, then mortar.** Your Second Brain might already be the first draft."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__from-note-to-book.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3030383884906769
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# Adding New Pages or New Sections"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "Hugo derives the URL from the content path under the language &#96;contentDir&#96;, and the template from section/kind/&#96;layout&#96;. Content goes under &#96;content/english/&#96; (root, project-setup)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "There are three distinct things you'll be asked for — pick the matching recipe:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "| Asking for...                                                   | It means...                                                           | Recipe   |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "| --------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "| \"Add a page like About\" (custom layout, image, hero, etc.)      | Content + a **dedicated template** selected via &#96;layout:&#96; frontmatter | Recipe 1 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "| \"Add a page like Elements / Privacy Policy\" (just text content) | Content only, rendered by the **existing generic template**           | Recipe 2 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "| \"Add a section like Call to Action\" (homepage block)            | A **content block** + **partial** wired into &#96;home.html&#96;              | Recipe 3 |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "## Where new/overridden layouts go — root &#96;layouts/&#96;, never &#96;themes/\u003ctheme\u003e/layouts/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "Hugo's union filesystem makes root take precedence over the theme for the **same relative path**. This is the standard Hugo way to customize a theme without forking it, and it's how this theme stays upgradeable:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "- **New templates, template overrides, new sections, new partials/components** → create them under root &#96;layouts/&#96; at the same relative path the theme would use (e.g. &#96;layouts/about.html&#96;, &#96;layouts/_partials/components/my-card.html&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "- **Never create or edit files directly under &#96;themes/\u003ctheme\u003e/layouts/&#96;** — those are vendored and reset/overwritten on theme updates (&#96;\u003cpm\u003e update-theme&#96; etc.)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- To override an existing theme template/partial, copy it from &#96;themes/\u003ctheme\u003e/layouts/...&#96; to the identical path under root &#96;layouts/...&#96;, then edit the copy."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- To add something brand new (a new partial, a new template, a new shortcode), just create it under root &#96;layouts/...&#96; directly — no theme copy needed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## Recipe 1 — Custom-templated page with frontmatter (like &#96;about&#96;)"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 23
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.agents/skills/hugo-template-guidance/references/adding-new-pages.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.30046749114990234
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "### Capture"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "Save what resonates - quotes, links, half-formed thoughts, screenshots, recipes, code snippets. The bar isn't \"might be useful someday.\" It's **\"this changes how I think or what I'd do.\"**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "Questions I ask before capturing:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "- What themes keep showing up in my work and life?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "- What high-value information do I already have access to?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "- What do I want to mix, match, and resurface later?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "Tools don't matter as much as **one trusted inbox**. TiddlyWiki, Obsidian, Notion, plain Markdown in a repo - pick something you'll actually open."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "### Organize"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "Don't build a perfect folder tree on day one. **Organize for action** - by project, area, or whatever you'll need when you sit down to work."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "If a note doesn't connect to something you're building, maintaining, or learning, it's archive bait."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "### Distill"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "Long notes are graveyards. Summarize in layers: headline, bullets, pull quotes. **Design notes for future-you** who's tired and in a hurry."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "Progressive summarization beats rewriting everything. Bold the good sentence today. Add a summary paragraph next month when you use it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "### Express"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "The point isn't hoarding. It's **shipping** - blog posts, recipes, client work, sermons, decks, code."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "Your Second Brain should make the blank page less blank. You're not starting from zero; you're assembling from parts you've already collected."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "## PARA: a simple organization lens"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "Another Forte lens I use alongside CODE:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "- **Projects** - things with a deadline"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "- **Areas** - ongoing responsibilities (health, finances, home)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "- **Resources** - topics of interest"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "- **Archives** - inactive stuff, out of the way but searchable"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "Not every note needs a perfect PARA home immediately. \"Is this active?\" is enough to start."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "## Why TiddlyWiki for my Second Brain"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "I wanted something **portable, ownable, and publishable**. TiddlyWiki is a single HTML file (or a small static site) that works offline, syncs through Git, and deploys free on Cloudflare Pages."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "That's the stack behind ideas.jorap.com: **TiddlyWiki + GitHub + Cloudflare Pages** - the same sustainable publishing idea I wrote about for JoRap Notes, applied to a private-public idea garden."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "Your stack might differ. The principle doesn't: **own the notes, own the export, own the publish path.**"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 85
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 32
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__building-a-second-brain.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.300396203994751
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Digital Serendipity\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Digital Serendipity - Stumbling on Your Own Ideas\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Serendipity isn't random luck - it's building a system where old notes surprise you at the right time.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Serendipity\", \"PKM\", \"Second Brain\", \"Linking\", \"Review\", \"Discovery\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"digital-serendipity\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "The best moments in my wiki: I search for one thing and find a note from two years ago that **solves today's problem.** That's digital serendipity - engineered luck through links, reviews, and resurfacing habits."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Algorithms won't do this for your private notes. You build the conditions."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Random note review"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Once a week I open an old tiddler from On This Day or random scroll. **Forgotten notes become new inputs.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Links create collisions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Dense linking means paths you didn't plan. Graph view isn't vanity - it's a **serendipity map** if you actually click around."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## RSS and small feeds"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Curated inputs beat infinite scroll. Unexpected connections often arrive from **one weird blog** you subscribed to on purpose."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Digital serendipity rewards maintenance. Link notes, review weekly, keep inputs narrow. **Your past self can collaborate with present you** if you leave trails."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__digital-serendipity.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.29608801007270813
    },

            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "## 🌟 What is this project?"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "JoRap Notes is a **personal blog and portfolio website** that shows off:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "- **Blog posts** about technology, productivity, and life experiences"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "- **Personal projects** and professional work"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "- **Resources and tutorials** for developers and tech enthusiasts"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "### Think of it like this:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "- **Hugo** = The engine that builds your website"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "- **Tailwind CSS** = The styling toolkit that makes it look good"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "- **Markdown** = The simple way you write your blog posts"
          }
        ],
