    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Daily Notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Daily Notes - Low-Stakes Logs That Compound\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Daily notes are my scratch pad for the day - tasks, fragments, links. Not a diary. Not permanent by default.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Daily Notes\", \"PKM\", \"Journaling\", \"Productivity\", \"Note Taking\", \"Review\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"daily-notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "I keep daily notes because **memory lies about what I did Tuesday.** A short log - what I worked on, what I read, what broke - makes weekly review honest."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Not a novel. Bullet fragments. Migrate anything important to project notes; let the rest fade."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## One file per day"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "&#96;2026-06-19.md&#96; or a dated tiddler. **No filing decisions.** Everything lands here first if I'm unsure where it goes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## End-of-day three lines"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "What shipped. What stuck. What's blocked. Ninety seconds. That's enough signal for Sunday review."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Link outward"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "If a daily bullet grows legs, it becomes its own atomic note with a link back. **Daily notes are staging**, not the archive."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Daily notes work when they're cheap to write and safe to ignore later. **Log the day. Promote the gold.** Don't polish what time will forget anyway."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__daily-notes.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.29484033584594727
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\u003c!--"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "Sync Impact Report:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "- Version change: 1.0.0 → 1.1.0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "- List of modified principles:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "  - Added Principle VI: Baseline Widely Available CSS"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "- Added sections: None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "- Removed sections: None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "- Templates requiring updates:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "  - ✅ .specify/templates/plan-template.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "- Follow-up TODOs: None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "--\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "# JoRap Notes Constitution"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "## Core Principles"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "### I. Content-First \u0026 Markdown-Centric"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "All content, especially blog posts and pages, MUST be created and managed as Markdown files (&#96;.md&#96;). The content is the core asset of the project, and the architecture must serve its presentation and management."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "### II. Static-First Architecture"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "The project MUST be a static site generated by Hugo. This principle ensures optimal performance, high security, and low hosting costs by avoiding server-side processing and databases for the frontend experience."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "### III. Convention Over Configuration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "The project MUST adhere to the established directory structure for content, assets, layouts, and configuration. This ensures predictability and simplifies maintenance. Any deviation from the standard structure requires justification."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "### IV. Performance \u0026 SEO as a Feature"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "All development and content creation MUST prioritize performance and Search Engine Optimization (SEO). This includes optimizing images, ensuring fast load times, using semantic HTML, and providing relevant metadata."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "### V. Automated \u0026 Multi-Platform Deployment"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "The project MUST support automated builds and deployments. It is designed with configurations for multiple platforms (AWS Amplify, Netlify, Vercel) to ensure flexibility and CI/CD best practices."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "### VI. Baseline Widely Available CSS"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "All CSS MUST be compatible with the \"Baseline\" of widely available CSS features. This means targeting features supported by the majority of browsers for at least 24 months. This ensures a consistent and accessible user experience for the widest possible audience, minimizing the need for polyfills or complex fallbacks. It prioritizes stability and broad compatibility over cutting-edge features."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "## Technical Specifications"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "The system is built on a modern, static-first technology stack:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 36
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.specify/memory/constitution.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.29335176944732666
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Note-Taking for Researchers\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Note-Taking for Researchers - Beyond Highlight Hell\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Researchers drown in PDF highlights. I borrow lit-review habits: source notes, synthesis notes, never merge the two.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Research\", \"Note Taking\", \"Academic\", \"Zettelkasten\", \"Citations\", \"PKM\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"note-taking-for-researchers\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "**Note-taking for researchers** needs layers: bibliographic record, summary of their argument, your critique, your synthesis. Highlighting without a synthesis note is **colored PDF, not knowledge.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Even if you're not academic, deep reading benefits from the same split."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## One note per source"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Facts and quotes stay tied to citation. **Never pollute synthesis with unverified paste.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Synthesis across sources"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Separate note: \"What I believe about X after reading A, B, C.\" **That's publishable material.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Zettelkasten for arguments"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Atomic claims with links to evidence notes. **Graph becomes argument map.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Researchers need defensible notes. **Separate source from opinion.** Your future paper (or post) will need both tracks clean."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__note-taking-for-researchers.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2909964323043823
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "├── 📁 themes/          # Website design and layout"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "│   └── jorap/          # Custom theme"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "│       ├── assets/         # Theme assets"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185,
            "text": "│       ├── layouts/        # HTML templates"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "│       └── static/         # Static theme files"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187,
            "text": "├── 📁 scripts/         # Build and maintenance scripts"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "├── 📁 static/          # Static files served as-is"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "├── 📁 public/          # Generated static site (build output)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "├── 📄 hugo.toml        # Main settings file"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "├── 📄 package.json     # Lists all tools and commands"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "└── 📄 README.md        # This file!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195,
            "text": "### Files beginners should know:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197,
            "text": "| File                          | What it does                             |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "| &#96;hugo.toml&#96;                   | Main website settings (title, URL, etc.) |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,
            "text": "| &#96;config/_default/params.toml&#96; | Website appearance settings              |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "| &#96;content/english/blog/&#96;       | Where your blog posts live               |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202,
            "text": "| &#96;data/theme.json&#96;             | Colors and fonts                         |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "| &#96;data/social.json&#96;            | Social media links                       |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204,
            "text": "| &#96;package.json&#96;                | Available commands and tools             |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 207
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 208,
            "text": "## 📝 How to Add Content"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 209
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 210,
            "text": "### Creating a New Blog Post"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 211
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 212,
            "text": "1. **Create a new file** in &#96;content/english/blog/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 213,
            "text": "2. **Name it** like &#96;my-awesome-post.md&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 214,
            "text": "3. **Add frontmatter** (metadata) at the top:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 215
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 216,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;markdown"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 217,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 218,
            "text": "title: \"My Awesome Post\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 219,
            "text": "description: \"A short description for SEO\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 220,
            "text": "date: 2024-01-01T10:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 221,
            "text": "author: \"Your Name\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 222,
            "text": "categories: [\"Technology\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 223,
            "text": "tags: [\"Hugo\", \"Web Development\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 224,
            "text": "image: \"images/my-post-image.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 225,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 226,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 227
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 228,
            "text": "# Your Post Content Goes Here"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 229
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 230,
            "text": "Write your blog post using **Markdown** syntax."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 231
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 232,
            "text": "## You can add subheadings"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 233
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 234,
            "text": "- Bullet points"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 235,
            "text": "- Are easy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 236
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 237,
            "text": "1. Numbered lists"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 238,
            "text": "2. Work too"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 239
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 240,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;code blocks are supported&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 241
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 242,
            "text": "![Images work too](images/my-image.jpg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 243,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 244
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 245,
            "text": "### Understanding Frontmatter"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 246
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 247,
            "text": "Frontmatter is the metadata at the top of your file:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 247
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 181
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.28999531269073486
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Hugo Page Bundles\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Hugo Page Bundles - Posts With Their Assets\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Page bundles keep images and Markdown together. I use them when a post has multiple files that shouldn't live in /static.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Website\", \"Technology\", \"Tutorial\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Hugo\", \"Page Bundles\", \"Content Management\", \"Static Sites\", \"Markdown\", \"Assets\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"hugo-page-bundles\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "A **Hugo page bundle** is a folder with &#96;index.md&#96; plus sibling assets - images, downloads, nested shortcodes. The post owns its files. No guessing which &#96;/images/&#96; filename matches which draft."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "I switch to bundles when a post has more than one custom image or an attachment."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Folder structure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "&#96;content/blog/my-post/index.md&#96; and &#96;hero.jpg&#96; beside it. Reference with &#96;![alt](hero.jpg)&#96; - **relative paths just work.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Leaf vs branch"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Leaf bundle: one page per folder. Branch bundle: section index with children. **Most blog posts are leaf bundles** when I bother."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Headless bundles"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Resources without their own URL - reusable image sets. Niche but handy for components."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Hugo page bundles reduce asset chaos. **Colocate what ships together.** For single-image posts, plain &#96;.md&#96; files are still fine."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__hugo-page-bundles.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2850415110588074
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Collaborative Knowledge\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Collaborative Knowledge - Shared Brains Without Shared Chaos\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Most of my notes are solo. But teams need shared context too - here's what I look for when knowledge has more than one author.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Collaboration\", \"PKM\", \"Knowledge Management\", \"Teams\", \"Documentation\", \"Second Brain\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"collaborative-knowledge\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "Personal PKM is freedom - your mess, your rules. **Collaborative knowledge** is politics plus documentation: who edits, what's canonical, how you avoid five conflicting versions of the onboarding doc."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "I've seen wikis die because nobody owned updates. I've seen Slack become the \"wiki\" and nothing was findable. Shared brains need shared habits, not just shared software."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## One canonical home"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Pick where truth lives - Notion, Confluence, Git repo, whatever. **Everything else links there.** Duplicate docs are how teams gaslight themselves."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Edit permissions match trust"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Open wiki sounds nice until someone deletes the runbook. I prefer **broad read, narrow write**, with suggestions or PRs for big changes - same energy as code review."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Personal notes still matter"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "My TiddlyWiki stays mine. Shared wiki holds agreed facts. **Draft thinking stays private** until it's ready to teach someone else."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Collaborative knowledge works when capture is easy, ownership is clear, and stale pages get archived without ceremony. **Shared context is a product** - treat maintenance like bug fixes, not a passion project."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__collaborative-knowledge.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2823469638824463
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Evergreen Notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Evergreen Notes - Ideas That Stay Fresh\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Evergreen notes are written to stay useful - timeless phrasing, clear claims, updated when reality changes.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Evergreen Notes\", \"PKM\", \"Note Taking\", \"Zettelkasten\", \"Second Brain\", \"Writing\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"evergreen-notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "Some notes expire - meeting minutes, this week's grocery run. **Evergreen notes** are the opposite: distilled ideas you expect to revisit for years. \"How I deploy Hugo to Cloudflare\" is evergreen if you keep it current. \"Tuesday standup\" is not."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Andy Matuschak's framing stuck with me: write notes you'd want to read again."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Timeless titles"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Name for the idea, not the date. **\"Middle-click tab workflow\"** beats \"Browser tip June 2026.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Maintain on use"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "When you touch an evergreen note for a project, **update it in the same session.** Five minutes now prevents wrong advice later."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Link heavily"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Evergreens become hubs. New fleeting notes point here; evergreen absorbs what still holds true."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Evergreen notes are your wiki's backbone. **Fewer, better, maintained.** They're how you stop re-researching the same thing every year."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__evergreen-notes.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2816767692565918
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"GTD vs PARA\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"GTD vs PARA - Tasks vs Knowledge\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"GTD runs my tasks. PARA runs my notes. They're cousins, not competitors - here's how I use both without doubling admin.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"GTD\", \"PARA\", \"Productivity\", \"PKM\", \"Tiago Forte\", \"David Allen\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"gtd-vs-para\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "**GTD** (Getting Things Done) is about trusted task capture and next actions. **PARA** (Projects, Areas, Resources, Archives) is about where knowledge lives relative to your life."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "I don't pick one religion. Tasks in a task app. Notes in PARA-ish folders/tags. Weekly review connects them."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## GTD for motion"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Inbox zero for tasks. Clarify next physical action. **\"Write PKM post\" becomes \"Open three tiddlers and outline H2s.\"**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## PARA for context"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Project notes beside project tasks conceptually. Area notes for ongoing responsibilities. **Resources for reference you'll link, not do.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## Weekly bridge"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Review projects → open linked notes → capture new tasks from what you read. **One session, both systems.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "GTD vs PARA isn't a fight. **GTD moves you. PARA orients you.** Use each where it's strongest."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__gtd-vs-para.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.28129759430885315
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Maps of Content\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Maps of Content (MOC) - Index Pages That Think\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"A MOC is a curated index of notes on a topic - not a folder list, a table of contents you write by hand.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/image-template.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"MOC\", \"Maps of Content\", \"PKM\", \"Linking\", \"Organization\", \"Second Brain\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"maps-of-content\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "**Maps of Content (MOCs)** are hub notes: \"PKM\" links to capture, PARA, zettelkasten, my failures. They're **narrative indexes** - ordered, annotated, alive."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Folders sort alphabetically. MOCs sort by what matters now."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## Start messy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "Brain dump links. Group later. **First MOC is ugly** - that's fine."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "## Add one line per link"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "Why this note belongs. **Context for future wanderers.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "## MOCs breed MOCs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "Top-level \"Ideas\" points to \"PKM\" and \"Hugo.\" **Fractal navigation.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "## Bottom line"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "Maps of content turn search into exploration. **Write the map when you have ten notes, not a hundred.** Maintenance beats archaeology."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "*Idea captured from [ideas.jorap.com](https://ideas.jorap.com/).*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/__maps-of-content.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.27998656034469604
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "[module]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "[[module.mounts]]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "source = 'assets'"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "target = 'assets'"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "[[module.mounts]]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "disableWatch = true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "source = 'hugo_stats.json'"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "target = 'assets/notwatching/hugo_stats.json'"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "############################# Outputs ############################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "[outputs]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130,
            "text": "home     = [\"HTML\", \"RSS\", \"WebAppManifest\", \"SearchIndex\", \"llms\", \"llmsfull\", \"md\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "page     = [\"HTML\", \"RSS\", \"md\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132,
            "text": "section  = [\"HTML\", \"RSS\", \"md\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "taxonomy = [\"HTML\", \"RSS\", \"md\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "term     = [\"HTML\", \"RSS\", \"md\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136,
            "text": "############################# Imaging ############################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "[imaging]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "# See https://github.com/disintegration/imaging"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "# Default JPEG or WebP quality setting. Default is 75."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140,
            "text": "quality = 80"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "resampleFilter = \"Lanczos\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "############################ Caches ##############################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144,
            "text": "[caches]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "[caches.images]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "dir = \":resourceDir/_gen\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "maxAge = \"720h\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "[caches.assets]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "dir = \":resourceDir/_gen\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "maxAge = \"720h\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "############################ Markup ##############################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "[markup]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "[markup.goldmark.renderer]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "unsafe = true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "# Keep the typographer extension on (curly quotes, ellipses, angle quotes) but"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "# render &#96;--&#96; and &#96;---&#96; as literal ASCII hyphens instead of en/em dashes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "[markup.goldmark.extensions.typographer]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "emDash = \"--\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "enDash = \"-\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "[markup.highlight]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "style = 'monokai' # see https://xyproto.github.io/splash/docs/all.html"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,

          },
          {

          },

syntax\n- callouts— Obsidian-style callout blocks\n- Latex— LaTeX math rendering\n- Mermaid diagrams— Diagram support via Mermaid\n- Syntax highlighting— Code block highlighting with themes\n- OxHugo compatibility— Support for ox-hugo Markdown\n- Roam Research compatibility— Support for Roam Research syntax\n- Citations— Academic citation support\n- Canvas— Render Obsidian Canvas files as interactive pages\n- Bases— Database-like views for your notes (tables, cards, galleries, and more)\n...\n- full-text search— Search across all your notes\n- graph view— Interactive graph visualization of note connections\n- backlinks— See which notes link to the current page\n- explorer— File tree sidebar for browsing notes\n- breadcrumbs— Breadcrumb navigation trail\n- table of contents— Per-page table of contents\n- folder and tag listings— Browse notes by folder or tag\n- recent notes— Display recently modified notes\n- popover previews— Hover previews for internal links\n- stacked pages— Andy Matuschak-style stacked sliding panes for tracing note connections\n- encrypted pages— Password-protect individual pages with client-side encryption\n...\n### Obsidian Compatibility\n...\n### OxHugo Compatibility\n...\n### Wikilinks\n...\nhttps://quartz.jzhao.\n...\nwikilinks\n...\n### Backlinks\n...\nhttps://quartz.jzhao.xyz/features/backlinks\n...\n### Graph View\n...\nhttps://quartz.jzhao.xyz/features/graph-view\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eObsidian Publish Alternatives: Simpler, Cheaper Options in 2026 | Unmarkdown™ Blog\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://unmarkdown.com/blog/obsidian-publish-alternatives\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eGraph view on the web. Obsidian's signature feature works on published sites. Visitors can explore connections between your notes visually.\n...\nQuartz is the closest feature match to Obsidian Publish. It's free, open-source, and produces a static site from your markdown files with graph view, backlinks, full-text search, and customizable themes.\n...\nWhat makes it compelling: Quartz delivers the signature Obsidian Publish features (graph, backlinks, search) without the subscription. The community is active, documentation is solid, and it supports wikilinks, tags, and most Obsidian markdown extensions.\n...\nThe tradeoff: These are web development tools. There's no out-of-the-box Obsidian integration, no graph view, no backlinks unless you build or install them yourself. The learning curve is steeper than any Obsidian-specific tool.\n...\nHugo/Jekyll/11ty\n...\n| Graph view | Yes | Yes | No | No (unless built) | Partial | No |\n...\n| Custom domain | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes (Pro) |\n...\n| Wikilink support | Yes | Yes | No | No | Yes | Strips to clean text |\n...\n| N/\n...\nUnmarkdown™ is not a digital garden publisher. It doesn't have graph view, backlinks, or the networked note experience that makes Obsidian Publish distinctive. It solves a different problem.\n...\nChoose Obsidian Publish if: You want the most polished digital garden experience, you rely on graph view and backlinks for your audience, and you don't mind the subscription cost. The tight integration with Obsidian makes setup nearly effortless.\n...\nChoose Hugo, Jekyll, or Eleventy if: You want full control over your site and are comfortable with web development. These give you the most flexibility at zero cost.\n...\nFor digital gardens and networked knowledge bases, Quartz delivers comparable features at zero cost. For documentation, MkDocs is more mature and widely supported. For multi-destination publishing (getting your markdown into the apps where people actually work), Unmarkdown™ stands alone.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eReview - Site Generators - Never Complete Only Abandoned - Obsidian Publish\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://publish.obsidian.md/xybre/permalink/7b636b0a-552c-48f7-98f9-01184647150f\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e]] and similar lightweight markup languages into full browseable static HTML files that can be deployed to a server without access to the original source files. # Markdown Plain Markdown to plain site. May supply some basic templating. - [[Hugo]] - [[Jekyll]] - [[werc]] # Digital Garden These create [[Obsidian Publish]]-style digital gardens. &#96;&#96;&#96;cardlink url: https://publishkit.dev/ title: \"KIT\" description: \"Publish full featured web apps with markdown\" host: publishkit.dev image: https://publishkit.dev/attachements/og.png &#96;&#96;&#96; &#96;&#96;&#96;cardlink url: https://blot.im/ title: \"Blot\" description: \"Turns a folder into a website\" host: blot.im favicon: https://cdn.blot.im/documentation/v-8d7d9d72/favicon-180x180.png &#96;&#96;&#96; ## Quartz 4 &#96;&#96;&#96;cardlink url: https://quartz.jzhao.xyz/ title: \"Welcome to Quartz 4\" description: \"Quartz is a fast, batteries-included static-site generator that transforms Markdown content into fully functional websites. Thousands of students, developers, and teachers are already using Quartz to publish personal notes, websites, and digital gardens to the web.\" host: quartz.jzhao.xyz favicon: ./static/icon.png image: https://quartz.jzhao.xyz/static/og-image.png &#96;&#96;&#96; \u003e Quartz is a fast, batteries-included static-site generator that transforms Markdown content into fully functional websites. - Obsidian compatibility, full-text search, graph view, note transclusion, wikilinks, backlinks, Latex, syntax highlighting, popover previews, Docker Support, and many more right out of the box - Hot-reload for both configuration and content - Simple JSX layouts and page components - Ridiculously fast page loads and tiny bundle sizes - Fully-customizable parsing, filtering, and page generation through plugins ## Misc &#96;&#96;&#96;cardlink url: https://github.com/MaggieAppleton/digital-gardeners title: \"GitHub - MaggieAppleton/digital-gardeners: Resources, links, projects, and ideas for gardeners tending their digital notes on the public interwebs\" description: \"Resources, links,\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003ehfactor/Grdn\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/hfactor/Grdn\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eObsidian-first Digital Garden Publishing template using Hugo.\n...\n# Grdn - A Hugo Theme for Digital Gardens\n\n \n\nGrdn is a clean, minimal Hugo theme specifically designed for publishing your [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/) notes as a beautiful digital garden. It maintains the integrity of your note connections while presenting them in a web-friendly format.\n...\n- Full support for &#96;[[wikilinks]]&#96; syntax\n - Hover previews for internal links\n - Backlinks section showing connections between notes\n - Graceful handling of broken links\n...\nfiltering for digital\n...\nworkflows\n - Shareable URLs that preserve filter state\n...\n- Flat URL structure regardless of folder hierarchy\n - All notes accessible under configurable base path\n - Support\n...\nfrontmatter metadata (title, category, growth stage)\n -\n...\n- [Hugo Extended](https://gohugo.io/installation/) (version 0.112.0 or later)\n- An [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/) vault (optional, but recommended)\n...\nPlace your markdown files in the &#96;content/notes/&#96; directory. If you're using Obsidian, you can set this folder as your vault.\n...\n1. **Visit** &#96;http://localhost:1313&#96; to see your digital garden!\n...\nNote content with [[wikilinks]] to other notes.\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;toml\n[params]\n  # Core Features\n  enableSearch = true           # Enable/disable search functionality\n  enableCategoryFilter = true   # Enable/disable category filter\n  enableGrowthFilter = true     # Enable/disable growth stage filter\n  \n  # Path Configuration\n  notesPath = \"/notes/\"         # Base path for all notes\n  \n  # UI Configuration\n  defaultTheme = \"system\"       # \"light\", \"dark\", or \"system\"\n  \n  # Site Information\n  description = \"My digital garden of interconnected notes\"\n  author = \"Your Name\"\n...\n- [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/) for the amazing note-taking experience\n- [Hugo](https://gohugo.io/) for the powerful static site generation\n- [Fuse.js](https://fusejs.io/) for client-side search capability\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eGrdn | Hugo Themes\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.gohugothemes.com/theme/hfactor-grdn/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eGrdn | Hugo Themes\n\n# Grdn\n\nTheme by Hfactor\n\n|\n\nUpdated: 26 Apr 2025\n\n|\n\n13 Stars\n\nObsidian-first Digital Garden Publishing template using Hugo.\n\nGithub\n\n### Categories\n\nDigital garden\n\n## Overview\n\nGrdn is an elegant and minimalist Hugo theme tailored for those looking to showcase their Obsidian notes as a beautifully formatted digital garden. This theme brings life to your notes while preserving the interconnectedness that makes them valuable, allowing users to navigate their thoughts seamlessly. With a focus on readability and organization, Grdn is perfect for anyone seeking a distraction-free zone to explore their ideas.\n\nNot only does Grdn maintain a sleek and modern design, but it also incorporates various features designed to enhance user interaction and experience. Whether you’re a seasoned developer or just starting your journey in digital gardening, Grdn provides the tools necessary to curate a thoughtful online space for your notes.\n\n## Features\n\n- Shareable URLs: URLs that maintain filter states allow for easy sharing of your digital garden with others.\n- Growth Stage Support: Customizable growth stages that help visually indicate the maturity of your notes, from initial thoughts to well-developed content.\n- Flat URL Structure: Ensures accessibility of all notes under a configurable base path for straightforward navigation.\n- Responsive Design: Fully compatible with all devices, providing a seamless viewing experience no matter the screen size.\n- Light/Dark Mode: Automatically adjusts to system preferences, ensuring a comfortable reading experience in any lighting.\n- Dynamic Navigation: Real-time updates with a dynamic filter bar and full-text title search for effortless exploration.\n- Backlinks Section: Easily see connections between your notes, enriching the context of your thoughts.\n- Obsidian Compatibility: Full support for [[wikilinks]] syntax and internal link previews, making navigation intuitive.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
This is an architecture question about implementing a notes section with full Obsidian functionality.

Three approaches are under consideration. Add Obsidian-style features directly to the existing Hugo site. Integrate Grdn patterns into the current theme. Or replace the separate TiddlyWiki garden with Quartz on a subdomain.

True Obsidian functionality cannot be replicated in a static Hugo site. Real-time editing, plugins, Canvas, daily notes, and live graph views are impossible without a local vault. The practical Hugo equivalents are wikilinks, backlinks, graph views, tags, callouts, and Mermaid diagrams.

A realistic notes architecture would include frontmatter properties, hover previews, aliases, and growth stages. The response should distinguish between feasibility and implementation, then ask whether to proceed and which features matter most.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Checking the blog layout and whether any wiki infrastructure already exists in the theme.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'll outline feasibility and architecture options for a notes folder with full Obsidian functionality.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Short answer: **you can get a strong Obsidian-*like reading experience* in a Hugo `notes/` section, but not “complete Obsidian functionality” on the web.** Obsidian is a local app with plugins, live editing, Canvas, daily-note templates, etc. Hugo is a static publisher. The right split is what you already describe in your draft posts: **Obsidian for thinking, Hugo for publishing.**

## What exists today

There is **no `content/english/notes/` folder yet**. The site has:

- **Blog** (`content/english/blog/`) — polished, dated posts
- **ideas.jorap.com** — your TiddlyWiki garden (separate from this repo)
- Search, Mermaid, TOC — already wired in the theme
- **No wikilinks, backlinks, or graph view** in Hugo yet

## What *can* live in `content/english/notes/`

These map well to a static Hugo section and match what you’d want from a digital garden:

| Feature | Hugo/static? | Notes |
|--------|--------------|-------|
| `[[wikilinks]]` | Yes | Goldmark render hook |
| Backlinks | Yes | Computed at build time |
| Graph view | Yes | Client-side JS from a JSON index |
| Tags / aliases | Yes | Frontmatter + permalinks |
| Full-text search | Yes | You already have the search module |
| Callouts, Mermaid, LaTeX | Mostly yes | Callouts need a hook or shortcode |
| Folder tree / MOCs | Yes | Section list + custom nav |
| Growth stages (seedling → evergreen) | Yes | Custom frontmatter |
| Hover previews | Yes | JS on internal links |
| Flat URLs (`/notes/slug/`) | Yes | Permalink config |

## What stays in Obsidian (not on Hugo)

- Real-time editing, plugins, Canvas, daily-note automation
- Private vault, local-first sync, encrypted notes
- Graph filters, workspaces, transclusion with live updates
- “Write in Obsidian, publish subset” — that workflow is the right model

Your own draft on this is basically the architecture:

> Obsidian: private vault, wikilinks, plugins. Hugo: static site, taxonomy, CDN deploy. **Think private, ship public.**

## Three realistic paths

### 1. Build a `notes/` section into JoRap (recommended if you want one site)

Add `content/english/notes/` plus theme overrides:

- `layouts/notes/single.html` — note layout with backlinks, tags, growth stage
- `layouts/_markup/render-link.html` — resolve `[[Note Title]]` to `/notes/slug/`
- `assets/js/notes-graph.js` — graph from a build-time `notes-index.json`
- `hugo.toml` — `[permalinks]` for flat note URLs
- Point Obsidian vault (or a subfolder) at `content/english/notes/` via Git

**Pros:** Same design as jorap.com, one deploy, fits your constitution (static-first, Markdown-centric).  
**Cons:** You build/maintain wikilink + graph + backlinks yourself (or borrow from Grdn).

### 2. Use [Grdn](https://github.com/hfactor/Grdn) patterns (Obsidian-first Hugo theme)

Grdn already ships wikilinks, backlinks, hover previews, growth stages, and search — aimed exactly at `content/notes/`.

**Pros:** Faster to a working garden.  
**Cons:** Either a second site/subdomain or porting Grdn’s logic into your `jorap` theme.

### 3. Quartz on ideas.jorap.com (replace or augment TiddlyWiki)

[Quartz](https://quartz.jzhao.xyz/) is the closest to “Obsidian Publish for free”: graph, backlinks, wikilinks, callouts, search.

**Pros:** Most complete out of the box.  
**Cons:** Separate stack from Hugo; not the same theme as jorap.com.

## Suggested architecture (if you want it on jorap.com)

```text
Obsidian vault  →  content/english/notes/  →  Hugo build  →  jorap.com/notes/
     ↑                        ↑
  private edits          git push = publish
```

- **Blog** = finished essays (`/blog/…`)
- **Notes** = linked garden (`/notes/…`)
- **Obsidian** = where you write; only notes you’re OK publishing go in that folder

MVP for “feels like Obsidian on the web”:

1. `notes/` section + flat permalinks  
2. Wikilink rendering  
3. Backlinks block on every note  
4. Graph page at `/notes/graph/`  
5. Include `notes` in site search  
6. Optional: `aliases` in frontmatter for `[[old name]]` links  

That’s not “complete Obsidian,” but it’s a real digital garden inside this repo.

---

**Which direction do you want?**

1. **Notes on jorap.com** — I can scaffold `content/english/notes/` and the wikilink/backlink/graph foundation in the existing theme.  
2. **Replace ideas.jorap.com** — Quartz or Grdn as a separate garden site.  
3. **Obsidian vault sync** — same as (1), but optimized so your vault folder *is* `content/english/notes/`.

If you pick (1) or (3), say which Obsidian features matter most (wikilinks, graph, backlinks, callouts, daily notes listing, etc.) and I’ll implement the first slice.

---

_**User**_

1. 
I will not be using obsidian. I want notes only seen on localhost. I want drafts to be seen on localhost only

---

The user wants a notes section on jorap.com. Obsidian is not used. Notes and drafts remain visible only on localhost.

Hugo will handle wikilinks and backlinks directly without Obsidian. A dedicated `content/english/notes/` section will support graph navigation. Draft visibility will be restricted to localhost using `hugo server -D`.

Environment-based Hugo checks will hide the notes section in production while keeping full wikilink, backlink, and graph rendering available only on localhost.

Four implementation paths were considered for localhost-only notes: draft flags, environment configs, template conditions, and disabled outputs. The simplest path is to rely on the existing dev script's `-D` flag, since `hugo server` already renders drafts automatically.

A dedicated `config/development/hugo.toml` will enable the notes section locally. Production builds will exclude it entirely. Blog drafts with the `__` prefix will also stay hidden from production.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning the implementation.

Evaluating Hugo strategies to keep notes and drafts visible only on localhost. Drafting all notes by default would hide them in production builds. Environment-based frontmatter conditionals are not natively supported.

The cleanest approach is to set all notes as drafts. Localhost development will use `hugo server --buildDrafts` to render them. Production builds will omit drafts entirely.

Blog drafts will follow the same draft strategy. Notes must be hidden from navigation menus, search indexes, sitemaps, RSS feeds, and LLM outputs. The `hugo server` command includes drafts by default, so the dev script needs no extra flags.

The notes section `_index.md` itself will be marked as draft. Navigation links will be restricted to development and server environments. Production preview builds will also exclude drafts since they run with the production environment flag.

A `params.toml` section will manage notes configuration. A dedicated `config/production/hugo.toml` may strip notes from production builds. Notes will live under `content/english/notes/` with custom layouts in `layouts/notes/`. Wikilinks will be handled through a goldmark extension or render hook.

Standard Hugo render hooks only handle standard markdown links, not custom wikilinks. A goldmark extension or post-processing regex would be required. Shortcodes are too manual. The best path forward is still being weighed.

Standard Hugo link render hooks cannot catch custom `[[wikilinks]]` syntax. Regex replacement on `.Content` inside templates is a fragile workaround. The Grdn theme will be examined for its wikilink implementation. A regex partial using `replaceRE` is being considered as an alternative.

A build-time note index will map titles to slugs. A partial template will resolve wikilinks by replacing bracketed text with links. The notes single template will pipe raw content through this partial before rendering.

Goldmark renders wikilinks as plain text in HTML output. This makes a post-processing partial viable. The template will iterate over regular pages and replace bracketed note references with actual links.

A concrete `notes/render-content.html` partial is being drafted. It will iterate over both published and draft notes to replace bracketed titles with live links. Draft notes will be included in the resolution scope.

The wikilink partial will generate anchor tags with a `wikilink` class and replace bracketed titles with live links. Aliases defined in note parameters will also be resolved. Draft notes will be included in this link resolution during local development. A backlink scan will identify notes referencing the current page title or slug, displayed at the bottom of each note page.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Search is limited to blog posts. Notes will stay out of search results.

Draft status will keep notes hidden from production. Development config will enable drafts and notes visibility. A notes link will appear in the header only outside production. Search may include notes during local development.

The development configuration will set `dev_only = true` under `[notes]` and override `include_sections` to include both blog and notes in search. Draft notes will be excluded from production builds entirely. The dev script will explicitly run `hugo server --buildDrafts`.

Notes will be accessible only through `npm run dev`. Preview and build commands will hide them entirely. Sample note files will be created under `content/english/notes/`.

A notes archetype will be added. Permalinks for notes will be configured in hugo.toml. Dedicated list, single, and graph templates will be created. Partial templates will handle content rendering, backlinks, and graph data. The header partial will be overridden to show a notes navigation link during local development. JavaScript and CSS assets will support the notes graph.

The search index template will be overridden to include notes only during local development. The graph view will use vanilla JavaScript with canvas or SVG, avoiding heavy dependencies. Wikilinks will resolve titles, aliases, and slugs. Backlinks will scan raw note content for references to the current page title, slug, or aliases.

A helper partial will fetch all notes pages. It will union regular pages with drafts when the server is active. Draft pages will be excluded in production builds.

Flat permalinks will use `/notes/:slugorfilename/` for note pages. The graph view will be a draft page at `content/english/notes/graph/index.md` with the graph layout. Wikilinks will be processed before Markdown rendering.

Goldmark renders wikilinks as literal text in HTML. The template will post-process note content by scanning for `[[title]]` patterns and replacing them with links to other notes. A regex will match titles and optional aliases. The template will iterate over all notes to build the replacement map.

The wikilink renderer will loop through every note page and replace `[[Title]]` patterns with anchor tags pointing to each note. It will skip the current page and use the note’s title and slug for links.

The wikilink renderer now handles aliases too. It replaces bare `[[slug]]` patterns and preserves `[[slug|alias]]` formatting. The final processed content is passed through `safeHTML`.

Alias replacements must be processed before plain title links to avoid incorrect matches. Unresolved wikilinks will be styled as broken links using a `wikilink-broken` class. The backlinks partial will collect references by scanning raw content for the current page title and slug.

The backlink scanner will gather the current page title, slug, and aliases into a list of search terms. It will scan raw content from every note page and record the parent page whenever any term appears. The logic needs fixing so a single match does not cause duplicate entries.

The backlink scanner will use a boolean flag to prevent duplicate entries when a page matches multiple search terms. Graph data will be generated inline within the graph template. The graph JavaScript will rely on a lightweight canvas-based force simulation without external libraries.

The search index template will be overridden to include notes only during local development. The header will be overridden with a minimal partial that appends a conditional notes link after the main menu items.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Implementing a localhost-only `notes/` section: drafts excluded from production builds, visible in `npm run dev`.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A bug was found in graph-data.html. The inner range uses `$.RelPermalink` incorrectly, causing it to reference the outer page instead of the intended scope.

The fix stores each note's permalink in a local variable before appending nodes and edges. The `notes/pages.html` partial is also being corrected to use `site` or an empty dictionary instead of the current context.

Hugo partials can return values using `{{- return $pages -}}`. The caller captures them with `{{ $notes := partial "notes/pages.html" . }}`.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Done. You now have a **localhost-only notes garden** — no Obsidian required.

## How it works

**Production (`npm run build`)** — notes and all `draft: true` content are **excluded**. Verified: no `/notes/` pages in `public/`.

**Local dev (`npm run dev`)** — shows notes, blog drafts (`__*.md`), and future-dated posts via `--buildDrafts --buildFuture`.

## What was added

| Piece | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `content/english/notes/` | Note files (all `draft: true`) |
| `[[wikilinks]]` | Link notes by title, alias, or filename |
| **Backlinks** | Shown at the bottom of each note |
| **Graph** | `/notes/graph/` — drag nodes to explore links |
| **Notes** nav link | Only appears when `hugo server` is running |
| Yellow banner | Reminds you content is local-only |

## Sample notes included

- [Getting Started](/notes/getting-started/) — how to use the system
- [Atomic Notes](/notes/atomic-notes/) — linked to Getting Started

## Create a new note

```bash
hugo new notes/my-idea.md
```

Or add a `.md` file under `content/english/notes/` with `draft: true`.

Link with `[[Other Note]]` or `[[Other Note|label]]`. Use `aliases:` in frontmatter for alternate link names.

## Publish a blog post

When ready for the world: move to `content/english/blog/`, remove the `__` prefix, set `draft: false`.

---

Run `npm run dev` and open **http://localhost:1313/notes/**. The Notes link appears in the nav only on localhost.

---

_**User**_

display the notes link at the footer only. the notes link is a 404

---

Done. Here's what was added:

**`/drafts/`** — a localhost-only index that lists unpublished blog posts in the same card grid as `/blog/` (sidebar, pagination, images, categories).

**Footer** — **Drafts** and **Notes** links appear at the bottom only while `hugo server` is running.

Draft posts still live in `content/english/blog/` with `draft: true` (and the `__` prefix). The drafts page queries those and renders them with the same `blog-card` component.

Restart with `npm run dev` if the server is already running, then open **http://localhost:1313/drafts/**. Production builds still exclude the whole section.

---

The banner is currently placed outside containers in layouts.

The fix belongs in the partial itself. It will wrap the banner in a container so all layouts inherit it automatically. Red styling will use semantic Tailwind classes for both light and dark modes.

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Updated the dev banner in two places:

1. **Container** — wrapped in `<section class="notes-dev-banner-wrap"><div class="container">…</div></section>` so it lines up with the rest of the page. Also moved it out of nested containers on note single/graph pages.

2. **Red styling** — light mode: red border/background (`#fef2f2` / `#dc2626`); dark mode: deep red background with lighter text.

It appears on `/notes/`, `/notes/graph/`, and `/drafts/` after a refresh.
